sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley lifts Prosus to “overweight,” sees 29% upside as discount peaks
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has upgraded Prosus to "overweight" from "equal-weight," setting a €51 price target and implying a 29% upside. This upgrade is attributed to Prosus's net asset value (NAV) discount reaching a historical high, with the stock trading at a 48% discount to Morgan Stanley's NAV. The discount to Tencent, which accounts for 85% of Prosus's NAV, has also widened.

feed.title: Harmony Biosciences stock faces pipeline scrutiny amid Polaryx integration challenges and rare disease market shifts
feed.summary: Harmony Biosciences is facing scrutiny over its pipeline following the 2025 acquisition of Epygenix Therapeutics, as Polaryx Therapeutics' recent 10-K filing reveals overlapping rare disease programs with their shared backer, Mstone Partners. US investors are closely monitoring how Harmony navigates integration challenges, competitive pressures, and the need to differentiate its assets in the neurology orphan drug market. The article highlights the strategic implications of these connections for Harmony's stock performance and resource allocation.

feed.title: U.S. Energy (USEG) proxy: Board seeks approval to issue ≥20% of shares to Roth
feed.summary: U.S. Energy Corp. (USEG) is seeking stockholder approval for several proposals at its 2026 Annual Meeting, including the election of two Class I directors, the ratification of its auditor, and an advisory vote on executive compensation. A key proposal asks stockholders to approve the issuance of common stock to Roth Principal Investments, LLC, which may exceed 20% of outstanding shares under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(d). The company also provided detailed information on executive and director compensation, corporate governance, and related party transactions.

feed.title: F5 and Forcepoint partner to secure enterprise AI from data creation to runtime operations
feed.summary: F5 and Forcepoint have allied to secure enterprise AI across its lifecycle, from data discovery and classification to runtime protection. This partnership addresses the challenges organizations face in securing sensitive data within AI systems and ensuring compliance. By combining Forcepoint's data security expertise with F5's application security and AI guardrails, they aim to provide continuous assurance and mitigate AI-related risks.

feed.title: Mark Cuban Calls $30,000 Corporate Healthcare Burden 'Insane,' Backs Bill To Break Up Big Medicine Giants UNH, CVS, CI
feed.summary: Mark Cuban argues that corporate healthcare costs are "insane," burdening employers with $30,000 per family annually and contributing to job cuts. He supports the "Break Up Big Medicine Act" to dismantle vertically integrated insurance companies like UnitedHealth, CVS Health, and Cigna, believing this will increase affordability and wages. The proposed legislation aims to prevent self-dealing by requiring these giants to divest conflicting subsidiaries, mirroring the 1933 Glass-Steagall Act.
